content.text:
Hi! I'll help you describe your practice so we can spin up a sched-linx scenario that matches how you actually run.

Let's start with a couple of orientation questions:

1. What kind of practice is it? (e.g. specialty consult clinic, urgent care, primary care, physical therapy, group therapy)
2. Roughly how big? (Number of providers, or number of patients you typically see in a day.)

Once I have that, we'll get into the specifics — the services you offer, whether you have rooms or equipment that constrain scheduling, and the pattern of how appointments are booked (scheduled per-provider, walk-in against capacity, or a mix).

I run a physical therapy practice and we also have a massage service category. 

We have 6 physical therapists, 2 of whom provide certain categories of massage as well, and 2 masseuses who do only massage services. we also have a special SoftWave service and 2 of our therapists are credentialed to do soft wave, one of which also does massage.

Great, that's a helpful picture! A physical therapy practice with massage services and a specialized SoftWave offering — and some providers who cross over between categories. Let me make sure I have the provider/service overlap right before we go further:

- **6 Physical Therapists** — all do PT; 2 of those also do massage; 1 of those 2 also does SoftWave
- **2 PTs credentialed for SoftWave** total — so 1 does SoftWave only (no massage), 1 does SoftWave + massage
- **2 Masseuses** — massage only

Does that sound right? And a couple of follow-ups:

1. **SoftWave** — is that equipment-dependent? (i.e., you have a specific SoftWave machine that only one patient can use at a time, and the credentialed provider has to be present?)
2. What are the **specific service types** you offer? For example, is PT broken into initial evaluations vs. follow-up visits? Are there different massage modalities (Swedish, deep tissue, etc.) that need to be scheduled differently?

1. yes, we have a single softwave machine. we keep it in a dedicated room, so only one appointment can be booked for that room at a time, and if it is in use softwave can't be done. 
2. yes, PT is broken up into initial eval, which is 1 hour, and regular, which is 40 mins. For massage, we offer 3 types: deep tissue, sports, and Swedish. Our two PT therapists who do massage only do sports. Sports can be 30 mins or 60 mins. Both deep tissue and Swedish can be 30, 60, or 90 minutes. All softwave appointments are 15 minutes.

This is shaping up really clearly. Let me reflect back the full picture before I start recording:

**Providers:**
- 6 Physical Therapists (PT1–PT6 for now — we can name them properly)
- 2 Masseuses
- 2 PTs are SoftWave-credentialed (one of those also does sports massage, the other doesn't do massage)
- 2 PTs do sports massage (one of whom is the SoftWave+massage PT above)

**Services:**
- PT Initial Eval — 60 min, requires specific provider
- PT Regular Visit — 40 min, requires specific provider
- Sports Massage — 30 or 60 min, requires specific provider
- Deep Tissue Massage — 30, 60, or 90 min, requires specific provider
- Swedish Massage — 30, 60, or 90 min, requires specific provider
- SoftWave — 15 min, requires specific provider + the dedicated SoftWave room

**One question on massage durations:** Since sports/deep tissue/Swedish come in multiple lengths, do patients pick the duration when booking (i.e., they're effectively separate services), or is the length determined some other way?

Also — before we go further — can you share the **actual names** of your providers? And do you have **one location or multiple**?

Good questions. Our masseuses are Artemis and Bianca. Our physical therapists are Cindy, Deon, Eli, Fern, Greg, and Hector. Cindy and Greg do massage. Deon and Greg do softwave. 
Patients pick the duration they want - they're effectively distinct services. We have one location and all our services are provided in person there.

Perfect, now I have the full picture. Let me map out the provider qualifications clearly:

- **Artemis** — Deep Tissue, Swedish, Sports massage
- **Bianca** — Deep Tissue, Swedish, Sports massage
- **Cindy** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular, Sports massage
- **Deon** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular, SoftWave
- **Eli** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular
- **Fern** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular
- **Greg** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular, Sports massage, SoftWave
- **Hector** — PT Initial Eval, PT Regular

Does that look right? One quick assumption I made: **Artemis and Bianca can do all three massage types** (Deep Tissue, Swedish, and Sports). Is that correct, or do they have their own specializations?

Also, what's the **name and timezone** of your clinic? That'll let me record the location and then we can talk about your typical weekly schedule.

Actually, Artemis doesn't do sports massage, only deep tissue and swedish, but everything else looks good. 
Our practice is called Muscle Works and we're located in Tulsa, OK, USA

Got it — Artemis is Deep Tissue and Swedish only, Bianca covers all three. And Tulsa, OK puts you in the **America/Chicago** timezone.

Let me go ahead and record everything we've established so far — location, all 8 providers, and all the services. I'll do it all at once!
Now let me wire up the SoftWave room and all the provider qualifications in one go:

Everything's recorded — location, all 8 providers, 11 services, the SoftWave room, and all qualifications. Here's a quick summary of what's wired up:

| Provider | PT Eval | PT Regular | Sports 30/60 | Deep Tissue 30/60/90 | Swedish 30/60/90 | SoftWave |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Artemis | | | | ✓ | ✓ | |
| Bianca | | | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | |
| Cindy | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
| Deon | ✓ | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Eli | ✓ | ✓ | | | | |
| Fern | ✓ | ✓ | | | | |
| Greg | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | | ✓ |
| Hector | ✓ | ✓ | | | | |

Now let's talk about your **schedule**. What are your clinic hours? And do all providers work the same hours, or do some work different days or shifts?

Our clinic is open 8-5, M-F. Closed weekends. We have rotating schedules Artemis is usually Monday-Wednesday, Bianca is usually Wedensday-Friday. Eli and Fern always open, and Hector and Cidy always close. Sometimes Deon opens. Sometimes Greg closes. We usually have between 5 and 6 people in between 9 and 4. There is a lot of variation though. We let most everybody set there own hours but just ask that they give us as much notices as possible so we know what services to make available.

That's a helpful picture! The self-set scheduling with advance notice makes a lot of sense for a practice like yours. Since individual schedules vary week to week, I'll record a **representative week** that reflects the typical patterns you described — the system can be adjusted from there as actual schedules come in.

Here's what I'm hearing as "typical":
- **Artemis**: Mon–Wed, full clinic hours (8–5)
- **Bianca**: Wed–Fri, full clinic hours (8–5)
- **Eli & Fern**: Mon–Fri, opening shift (8–1, roughly?)
- **Hector & Cindy**: Mon–Fri, closing shift (12–5, roughly?)
- **Deon**: A few days opening (let's say Mon/Wed/Fri as a typical pattern?)
- **Greg**: A few days closing (let's say Tue/Thu?)

A couple of questions before I lock this in:

1. When you say "open" and "close" — are you thinking something like **8am–1pm for openers** and **12pm–5pm for closers** (with an overlap in the middle)? Or is it more like strict half-days with no overlap?
2. For Deon and Greg, do you want me to pick a representative few days, or would you rather I just record them for the full week since their schedules vary anyway?

1. Yes, they can work all day if they like, but most who open tend to leave a little earlier, and those who close start a little later. 
2. You can put Deon and Greg on for the full week, as long as we can make adjustments and have the availability reflected on the booking calendar we surface to patients we're happy.

Perfect. I'll record a representative week of **July 20–25, 2026** (Monday–Friday). I'll use:

- **Artemis**: Mon–Wed 8am–5pm CT
- **Bianca**: Wed–Fri 8am–5pm CT
- **Eli & Fern**: Mon–Fri 8am–1pm CT (typical opener pattern)
- **Hector & Cindy**: Mon–Fri 12pm–5pm CT (typical closer pattern)
- **Deon & Greg**: Mon–Fri 8am–5pm CT (full week, adjustable)

CT (America/Chicago) is UTC-5 in July, so 8am CT = 13:00 UTC and 5pm CT = 22:00 UTC. Let me record all of these at once!
